system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet straight duct (along +x)
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (  0  25 -25)   // 1
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 3
    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5 (same as 1)
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6 (same as 2)
    (-50 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend roof connecting block 0 to bend
    (  0  25  25)   // 8 (same as 4)
    (  0  25 -25)   // 9 (same as 5/1)
    ( 25  25 -25)   // 10
    ( 25  25  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - bend wall (goes down in -y)
    (  0 -25  25)   // 12 (same as 7)
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 13 (same as 6/2)
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 15

    // Block 3 - outlet straight duct extending in -y
    (  0 -125  25)  // 16
    (  0 -125 -25)  // 17
    ( 25 -125 -25)  // 18
    ( 25 -125  25)  // 19
    (-500 -50  25)  // 20
    (-500 -50 -25)  // 21
    (-500 -125 -25) // 22
    (-500 -125  25)  // 23
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight duct (along +x)
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend region
    hex (4 5 10 11 7 6 13 12) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Post-bend duct
    hex (12 13 15 14 16 19 18 17) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Outlet duct
    hex (20 23 22 21 16 17 23 22) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (20 21 22 23)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 1 5 4)   // front wall (+z)
            (0 3 2 1)   // back wall (-z)
            (1 2 6 5)   // left wall (-y)
            (4 5 6 7)   // right wall (+y)

            // Block 1 walls
            (8 9 10 11) // front wall (+z)
            (8 11 9 10) // back wall (-z)
            (4 8 11 7)  // left wall (-y)
            (5 6 10 9)  // right wall (+y)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 13 14 15) // front wall (+z)
            (12 15 14 13) // back wall (-z)
            (12 13 19 16) // left wall (-y)
            (15 14 10 11) // right wall (+y)

            // Block 3 walls
            (20 23 22 21) // front wall (+z)
            (20 16 17 21) // back wall (-z)
            (20 23 17 22) // left wall (-y)
            (21 22 17 16) // right wall (+y)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //
')]</foamfiles>

    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (12 13 15 14 16 19 18 17) ... has inward-pointing faces`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Error 1 Deep Dive

The error is:
```
Block hex (12 13 15 14 16 19 18 17) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(14 17 18 15) 4(16 19 18 17)
```

The block definition `hex (12 13 15 14 16 19 18 17)` produces inward-pointing normals. Let me trace through the vertex coordinates:

- 12: (0, -25, 25)
- 13: (0, -25, -25)
- 15: (25, -50, 25)
- 14: (25, -50, -25)
- 16: (0, -125, 25)
- 19: (25, -125, 25)
- 18: (25, -125, -25)
- 17: (0, -125, -25)

The bottom face is `(12 13 15 14)` = [(0,-25,25), (0,-25,-25), (25,-50,25), (25,-50,-25)]
The top face is `(16 19 18 17)` = [(0,-125,25), (25,-125,25), (25,-125,-25), (0,-125,-25)]

The issue is that the **bottom face slopes downward in y** (from y=-25 to y=-50) while the **top face is flat at y=-125**. The vector from bottom-face-center to top-face-center is `(0, -100, 0)`, which is parallel to the bottom face's normal direction. This makes the block non-convex in terms of the right-hand rule application.

### Recommended Fix

The problem is the **ordering of vertices in the hex block**. Swap the vertex ordering so that the bottom face winds counterclockwise when viewed from outside and the top face winds counterclockwise too. Try:

**Block 2:** `hex (14 15 19 18 13 12 16 17)`

This reverses the winding order. More precisely, the correct approach for this L-shaped post-bend block is to split it differently. Actually, the best fix is to **redefine Block 2 entirely** to maintain consistent right-hand rule winding:

**Block 2:** `hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19)`

Wait — let me reconsider the geometry. The real issue is that vertices 12 and 13 are at y=-25 while vertices 16-19 are at y=-125, and vertices 14 and 15 are at y=-50. The block spans from y=-25 down to y=-125 with a "step" at y=-50. The correct winding should place the "bottom" (lowest y) face as the top face in the vertex list.

Try: **Block 2:** `hex (16 17 18 19 12 13 14 15)`

No — the fundamental problem identified in Attempt 4 is that this block shape is geometrically invalid for a single hexahedron because it has a "step". The block needs to be split differently.

### Key Insight

The block topology needs to be restructured so that **no single hex block spans the full L-corner**. Instead, use a 4-block approach where the bend is handled by smaller intermediate blocks. Specifically:

- **Block 0**: Inlet duct along +x: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`
- **Block 1**: Upper bend arm (x=0..25, y=-25..25): `hex (4 5 10 11 7 6 13 12)`
- **Block 2**: Lower bend arm transition (x=0..25, y=-50..-25): `hex (7 6 14 15 3 2 13 12)` — Wait, vertex 3/2 are at x=-50, not x=0.

Actually, the simplest and most robust fix: **Adopt the exact blockMeshDict from the squareBend tutorial verbatim.** The tutorial already has a working 4-block decomposition for this exact geometry. Copy the tutorial's `blocks` and `boundary` sections exactly, keeping only the vertex coordinates from your current file. The tutorial's block definitions are proven to work.

## Error 2

Cascading from blockMesh failure. Resolves once blockMesh succeeds.

## Summary

1. **Replace the entire `blocks` section** in `blockMeshDict` with the proven squareBend tutorial block definitions. The tutorial's 4-block topology is the gold standard here — it correctly handles the bend with properly wound hexahedra.

2. **Keep all vertex coordinates (0-23)** as they are, since those are geometrically sound.

3. **Update all boundary face definitions** in `blockMeshDict` to match the tutorial's patch naming and face connectivity.

4. **Re-run `blockMesh`** before attempting `rhoSimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>

Replace the walls boundary sub-dictionary faces with: Block 0 walls (0 1 5 4 front +z, 0 3 2 1 back -z, 1 2 6 5 left -y, 4 5 6 7 right +y); Block 1 walls (4 8 11 7 left -y, 5 6 10 9 right +y, 8 9 10 11 front +z, 8 11 9 10 back -z); Block 2 walls (12 13 14 15 front +z, 12 15 14 13 back -z, 12 13 19 16 left -y, 15 14 10 11 right +y); Block 3 walls (20 23 22 21 front +z, 20 16 17 21 back -z, 20 23 17 22 left -y, 21 22 17 16 right +y). Keep inlet as patch with face (0 4 7 3) and outlet as patch with face (20 21 22 23).'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
